concerns.analysis: The output contains an email address string that is a concatenation of two distinct email addresses from the source. In the source, one email is struck through and another is underlined as an amendment. The conversion process merged them into a single, invalid email address string. According to the criteria, any mismatch in contact information (including merged strings that create a new, non-existent entity) is a critical failure.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The document is a legal amendment where strikethrough and underline markings are used to denote changes in law. The output fails to preserve or mark these distinctions, instead presenting both the deleted text and the new text as a single continuous string (e.g., in the title, paragraph (a), and list item (3)). concerns.output_quote: "...search from the appropriate governmental authority... 74C-8.1(a);reporting service designated... for any state where the applicant has resided within the preceding 60 months;"

concerns.analysis: The source PDF is a legal document showing proposed amendments, using strikethroughs to indicate deletions and underlines to indicate additions. The HTML output completely removes these visual formatting cues. This results in a document where deleted and added text are merged together without distinction (e.g., 'APPLICATION/ARMED ARMORED CAR' and the concatenated email addresses).
